data.narrative:
Twenty, a Series A defense and security company founded by Joe Lin (former VP at Palo Alto Networks and founder of Expanse National Security Division), has raised $38M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 peers across Defense & Security: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, including same-stage comparables Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and NODA AI ($125M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $38M total raised as a proxy valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a valuation of $76M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $225M, with a range of $125M to $500M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $65M to $259M, with a median of $150M. The wide spread reflects material heterogeneity within the Defense & Security peer set at Series A stage—a 4x gap between floor and ceiling valuations. Key uncertainties include Twenty's differentiation versus higher-valued peers like Stark Defence, revenue traction not visible in this dataset, and the applicability of public market comparables to early-stage private defense contractors. Stage-progression yields a conservative floor; peer multiples anchor the upside.
